mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate declines are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that compresses corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in over 60% of synchronized expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% per month, or 5-day forward returns during tightening are negative in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.0% annualized, confirming robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicate a synchronized economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of synchronized months, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth decelerates while the federal funds rate rises, it signals demand fragility under tighter financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where this divergence occurs, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance during periods of stable monetary policy.
